seo_title: Towards Generalization-Oriented Models for Vehicle Routing Problems…
meta_description: A new approach to improve cross-distribution generalization in vehicle routing problems using Residual Refined Experts with Instance-level Gating.
body_html:
What is the optimal set of routes for a fleet of vehicles to traverse in order to deliver to a given set of customers?

Improving Cross-Distribution Generalization

Residual Refined Experts with Instance-level Gating (R2E-IG) improves cross-distribution generalization by partitioning the policy network into multiple modules and adaptively recombining modules to form specific policies during inference. The proposed architecture enhances expert expressiveness via residual refinement, learns distribution-aware instance representations, and dynamically reweights training data from different distributions to emphasize more informative ones.

Introduces a Residual Refined Expert (R2E) architecture that enhances expert expressiveness via residual refinement. Designs an instance-level gating mechanism that learns distribution-aware instance representations and routes inputs to suitable modules. Proposes a mixed-distribution training mechanism equipped with Dynamic Weight Adaption (DWA), which dynamically reweights training data from different distributions to emphasize more informative ones.

Performance on Synthetic and Benchmark Datasets

R2E-IG Competitive performance against state-of-the-art baselines on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution instances.

DWA Dynamically reweights training data from different distributions to emphasize more informative ones, improving generalization.

source.title: Towards Generalization-Oriented Models for Vehicle Routing Problems with Mixture-of-Experts
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to improve the performance of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) models on Vehicle Routing Problems (VRPs), which are commonly used in logistics and transportation. The proposed model, called Residual Refined Experts with Instance-level Gating (R2E-IG), uses a modular architecture that adapts to different distributions during inference. This approach enables the model to generalize better across different scenarios and datasets. Experimental results show that R2E-IG outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution instances.
